summary: Reference‑quality audience recording with a massive soundstage; the show shines especially on Eyes, Bird Song, Let It Grow and Black Peter.
review: The FOB recording is praised as a reference‑grade capture – deep, tall and wide with an impressive stereo image (aside from a fixable phase‑inverted channel). Musically the concert is lauded for its strong second set, seamless flow and standout performances of Eyes, Bird Song, Let It Grow, Truckin' and Black Peter, though a few listeners note Jerry’s timing was a bit loose on the first set and Let It Grow feels slightly “phoned‑in” to one reviewer.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 4–5 stars, highlighting both superb audio fidelity and a high‑energy performance; only minor criticisms (timing, one mixed song) keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Huge, immersive soundstage with crystal‑clear ambience; one channel was phase‑inverted (fixable). No dropouts or major noise issues reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set mixes rock, blues and folk; second set is laid‑back, nuanced and transformational, delivering a seamless, high‑energy arc that many fans call the highlight of the night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Tone described as massive and reference‑grade; timing/attack a bit off in first set but delivers smooth Bird Song and restrained, tasteful playing later.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir is noted to be in fine form throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Sublime drum fill on Truckin' praised; overall drumming solid.
